summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a rain‑kissed Rochester show, highlighted by a stellar Box of Rain opener and blazing Green Onions/China Cat > Rider sequence.
review: The recording is an ultra‑premium soundboard mix that delivers crisp, balanced audio with only occasional quirks (a brief electronic drum glitch). The performance itself is high‑energy – the band rides a rainy start into a flawless first set and a second set that shines on Green Onions, China Cat > Rider and a psychedelic The Other One, making it one of 1988’s standouts.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~3.9/5, but the overwhelming majority praise both the show and the soundboard quality; weighting toward performance (70%) pushes the overall AI score into the very good range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with superb instrument separation. Reviewers repeatedly call it “ultra‑premium” and note only minor oddities such as a brief electronic drum effect at the start of Samson.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rain‑stopped just as Box of Rain opened, setting a magical mood. The first set stayed tight and song‑focused, while the second set “shines” with Green Onions, China Cat > Rider and an intense The Other One jam, delivering a consistent high‑energy arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crisp, blazing tone; even noted wearing a blazer. Delivers “sweet Jerry tunes” and strong vocal delivery.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) singled out as being on his A‑game – Far From Me solo, Green Onions groove, overall stellar playing.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally solid; a brief comment about experimental electronic effects at the start of Samson but otherwise “blazing” especially in the Green Onions → Drums transition.
